title: Validate balanced brackets
planQuestions: What information must remain available when a closing bracket appears?
How can nesting order be represented?
Which crossed example would prove the order matters?

hints.text: A closing bracket must match the latest unmatched opening bracket.
hints.concept: Nested pairs are resolved last-opened, first-closed.

hints.text: What small collection naturally gives back the most recent opening bracket?
hints.concept: The order of unresolved openers matters.
hints.lookAt: The state tracked while scanning each character.

hints.text: Inspect the function's single numeric depth counter.
hints.concept: One depth value cannot remember bracket kinds.
hints.lookAt: The loop that handles opening and closing characters.
hints.testIdea: Compare a valid nested string with the crossed sequence ([)].

explainBackQuestion: Why does matching brackets require remembering order as well as quantity?

brief.story: A note formatter accepts ordinary text with grouping markers. It must reject crossed pairs such as ([)] while allowing words and punctuation around valid groups.
brief.desiredBehavior: Return whether parentheses, square brackets, and braces are correctly paired and nested, ignoring other characters.
acceptanceCriteria: All three bracket kinds are supported.
A closing bracket must match the most recent opener.
constraints: Work in the TypeScript Kata Lab library.
Return a boolean without changing the input text.
